API Routers¶

auth¶

Authentication API routes.

This module exposes endpoints for registration, login, token refresh, email confirmation, and password reset.

async src.api.auth.confirm_password_reset(body: ConfirmPasswordReset, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → MessageResponse[source]¶

Verify the reset token and update the user’s password.

Parameters:

  • body – Request body containing the reset token and the new password.

  • db – Async DB session.

Raises:

HTTPException 400 – If the token is invalid, expired, or user not found.

Returns:

Confirmation message.

async src.api.auth.confirmed_email(token: str, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → MessageResponse[source]¶

Confirm a user’s email address using a verification token.

Parameters:

  • token – JWT email-verification token from the confirmation link.

  • db – Async DB session.

Raises:

HTTPException 400 – If the user is not found.

Returns:

Confirmation message.

async src.api.auth.login_user(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(dependency=None, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Token[source]¶

Authenticate credentials and return an access_token/refresh_token pair.

On successful authentication the refresh token is stored in the DB for rotation and revocation, and the user cache is invalidated so subsequent requests read the latest user state.

Raises:

HTTPException 401 – If credentials are invalid or the account is unconfirmed.

Returns:

A Token containing access_token, refresh_token, and token_type.

Example

curl -X POST “{base_url}/auth/login” -H “Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded” -d ‘username=alice&password=secret’

async src.api.auth.logout(db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user=Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → MessageResponse[source]¶

Revoke the current user’s refresh token and clear their cache entry.

Parameters:

  • db – Async DB session.

  • current_user – Authenticated user from JWT.

Returns:

Confirmation message.

async src.api.auth.refresh_tokens(body: TokenRefreshRequest, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Token[source]¶

Exchange a valid refresh token for new access + refresh tokens.

Parameters:

  • body – Request body containing the refresh token.

  • db – Async DB session.

Raises:

HTTPException 401 – If the refresh token is invalid, expired, or revoked.

Returns:

New JWT access token, new refresh token, and token type.

Example

curl -X POST “{base_url}/auth/refresh” -H “Content-Type: application/json” -d ‘{“refresh_token”:”<your_refresh_token_here>”}’

async src.api.auth.register_user(user_data: UserCreate, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, request: Request, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → UserResponse[source]¶

Register a new user and enqueue an email verification send.

The endpoint hashes the provided password, creates the user record, and schedules send_email as a background task so the HTTP request is not blocked by network I/O.

Raises:

HTTPException 409 – If the email or username is already taken.

Returns:

The created UserResponse object (without raw password).

Example

curl -X POST “{base_url}/auth/register” -H “Content-Type: application/json” -d ‘{“username”:”alice”,”email”:”alice@example.com”,”password”:”secret”}’

async src.api.auth.request_password_reset(body: RequestPasswordReset, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, request: Request, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → MessageResponse[source]¶

Send a password reset email if the given address is registered.

Always returns 200 to avoid leaking whether the email exists.

Parameters:

  • body – Request body with the user’s email.

  • background_tasks – FastAPI background tasks runner.

  • request – Incoming HTTP request (used to build base URL).

  • db – Async DB session.

Returns:

Generic confirmation message.

Example

curl -X POST “{base_url}/auth/reset-password/request” -H “Content-Type: application/json” -d ‘{“email”:”alice@example.com”}’

users¶

User-related API routes.

Provides endpoints for retrieving the current user and updating avatars.

async src.api.users.me(request: Request, user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → UserResponse[source]¶

Return the profile of the currently authenticated user.

Parameters:

  • request – Incoming HTTP request (required by SlowAPI rate limiter).

  • user – Current user resolved from JWT + Redis cache.

Returns:

User profile data.

Example

curl -H “Authorization: Bearer <access_token>” “{base_url}/users/me”

async src.api.users.update_avatar(file: UploadFile = File(PydanticUndefined), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_role.<locals>._check>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → UserResponse[source]¶

Upload a new avatar image for the current user. Admin only.

Regular users receive 403 Forbidden.

Parameters:

  • file – The image file to upload.

  • current_user – Admin user resolved from JWT dependency.

  • db – Async DB session.

Raises:

  • HTTPException 400 – If no file is provided or it is not an image.

  • HTTPException 403 – If the user is not an admin, or Cloudinary rejects the upload.

  • HTTPException 502 – If Cloudinary upload fails for another reason.

Returns:

Updated user profile with new avatar URL.

Example

curl -X PATCH “{base_url}/users/avatar” -H “Authorization: Bearer <access_token>” -F “file=@/path/to/avatar.jpg”

contacts¶

Contacts API router — CRUD operations for user contacts.

async src.api.contacts.create_contact(body: ContactCreate, user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Create a new contact for the current user.

Example

curl -X POST “{base_url}/contacts/” -H “Authorization: Bearer <access_token>” -H “Content-Type: application/json” -d ‘{“name”:”Bob”,”email”:”bob@example.com”,”phone”:”+123456789”}’

async src.api.contacts.delete_contact(contact_id: int, user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶
async src.api.contacts.get_contact(contact_id: int, user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶
async src.api.contacts.get_contacts(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100, user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶

Return user contacts with optional pagination.

Example

curl -H “Authorization: Bearer <access_token>” “{base_url}/contacts/?skip=0&limit=20”

async src.api.contacts.search_contacts(q: str = Query(PydanticUndefined), user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶
async src.api.contacts.upcoming_birthdays(user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶
async src.api.contacts.update_contact(contact_id: int, body: ContactUpdate, user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]¶
